question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R7 (the secondary RR) re-points R6's neighbor from the RR-CLIENT
neighbor-group to the RR-PEER neighbor-group. R7 now treats R6 as a
regular iBGP peer (no `route-reflector-client`). R6-originated prefixes
still get reflected by R3 (the primary RR) — so initially nothing
breaks visibly. But if R3 is restarted or its session to R6 is
disrupted, only R7's reflection path exists, and that path no longer
reflects to other clients. Redundancy is silently halved.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.107 (R7, secondary RR), the R6 neighbor was moved from the RR-CLIENT neighbor-group to RR-PEER, so R7 no longer reflects R6-originated prefixes to other clients.
R3 (primary RR) still reflects R6's routes, so nothing breaks visibly, but redundancy is silently halved - if R3 or its R6 session is disrupted, R7's surviving path no longer reflects R6's prefixes to the rest of the AS.
Fix: move R6 back to the RR-CLIENT neighbor-group on R7.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.6.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.7.
- R6: neighbor 192.0.2.3.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R6 (1 instance):
- R6: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.6
 no use neighbor-group RR-CLIENT
 use neighbor-group RR-PEER

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.6
 no use neighbor-group RR-PEER
 use neighbor-group RR-CLIENT

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:35:00.326 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.6 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:35:09.747 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.7 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:35:00.325 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.3 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:35:00.325 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
